items.title: Venezuela earthquakes: at least 32 dead, 700 injured and dozens of buildings collapsed, says interim president – latest updates
items.standfirst: Delcy Rodríguez says rescuers from others countries arriving in Venezuela over coming hours after two powerful quakes hit capitalVenezuela earthquakes: back-to-back quakes collapse buildings in capital CaracasVolunteers, medics and relatives of victims have raced to the Altamira area in Caracas hoping to help save survivors from the rubble of collapsed buildings there.“I live far away [but] ... I came here riding my motorbike as fast as I could,” said José Morillo, as he arrived outside a block of flats called Residencias Obelisco. Continue reading...
items.content: Delcy Rodríguez says rescuers from others countries arriving in Venezuela over coming hours after two powerful quakes hit capitalVenezuela earthquakes: back-to-back quakes collapse buildings in capital CaracasVolunteers, medics and relatives of victims have raced to the Altamira area in Caracas hoping to help save survivors from the rubble of collapsed buildings there.“I live far away [but] ... I came here riding my motorbike as fast as I could,” said José Morillo, as he arrived outside a block of flats called Residencias Obelisco. Continue reading...

items.title: Thursday briefing: Why EU​-Taliban talks have sparked outrage among Afghan women ​
items.standfirst: In today’s newsletter: Amid rising anti‑immigration rhetoric across Europe, the decision to engage with the Taliban signals a profound shift in how the EU balances security and human rightsGood morning. It’s a slap in the face. That’s the phrase I kept hearing – in furious overnight messages, in blazing opinion columns – as Afghan women responded to the meeting between EU officials and the Taliban that took place in Brussels on Tuesday.The talks, to discuss how to scale up the deportation of Afghan migrants, were met with widespread outrage, and disbelief that Europe would countenance offering legitimacy to a regime that affords a bird better protections than a woman.World news | Venezuela’s interim leader has declared a state of emergency after the country was struck by two powerful earthquakes that collapsed dozens of buildings and killed at least 32 people, with experts warning the death toll could rise significantly.Heatwave | The UK has broken its all-time temperature record for June and France has recorded its hottest day ever for the second day running, as a heatwave affecting more than 90 million people sweeps across swathes of Europe.UK politics | Donald Trump has labelled Andy Burnham “extremely liberal”, in his first public comments about the former Greater Manchester mayor since he emerged as the frontrunner to replace Keir Starmer.Europe news | The first case of Ebola has been confirmed in France, the country’s health ministry has said, in a doctor who had returned from a humanitarian mission to an area affected by the outbreak in the DRC.UK news | A little-known system in which US military personnel are tried through a court martial for alleged crimes committed in the UK is under growing scrutiny. Continue reading...
items.content: In today’s newsletter: Amid rising anti‑immigration rhetoric across Europe, the decision to engage with the Taliban signals a profound shift in how the EU balances security and human rightsGood morning. It’s a slap in the face. That’s the phrase I kept hearing – in furious overnight messages, in blazing opinion columns – as Afghan women responded to the meeting between EU officials and the Taliban that took place in Brussels on Tuesday.The talks, to discuss how to scale up the deportation of Afghan migrants, were met with widespread outrage, and disbelief that Europe would countenance offering legitimacy to a regime that affords a bird better protections than a woman.World news | Venezuela’s interim leader has declared a state of emergency after the country was struck by two powerful earthquakes that collapsed dozens of buildings and killed at least 32 people, with experts warning the death toll could rise significantly.Heatwave | The UK has broken its all-time temperature record for June and France has recorded its hottest day ever for the second day running, as a heatwave affecting more than 90 million people sweeps across swathes of Europe.UK politics | Donald Trump has labelled Andy Burnham “extremely liberal”, in his first public comments about the former Greater Manchester mayor since he emerged as the frontrunner to replace Keir Starmer.Europe news | The first case of Ebola has been confirmed in France, the country’s health ministry has said, in a doctor who had returned from a humanitarian mission to an area affected by the outbreak in the DRC.UK news | A little-known system in which US military personnel are tried through a court martial for alleged crimes committed in the UK is under growing scrutiny. Continue reading...

items.title: See the aftermath of the Venezuela earthquakes
items.standfirst: Two earthquakes hit Venezuela on Wednesday, the first measuring a magnitude of 7.2 and the second 7.5. The country's acting President, Delcy Rodriguez, declared a state of emergency. Videos show the heavy damage in the capital of Caracas as rescue crews look for survivors.
items.content: Two earthquakes hit Venezuela on Wednesday, the first measuring a magnitude of 7.2 and the second 7.5. The country's acting President, Delcy Rodriguez, declared a state of emergency. Videos show the heavy damage in the capital of Caracas as rescue crews look for survivors.

items.title: At least 32 killed, 700 injured in 2 major earthquakes in Venezuela, says acting president
items.standfirst: A 7.2-magnitude earthquake and a 7.5-magnitude were less than a minute apart, said the U.S. Geological Survey. The second earthquake was the largest to hit the country since 1900.
items.content: A 7.2-magnitude earthquake and a 7.5-magnitude were less than a minute apart, said the U.S. Geological Survey. The second earthquake was the largest to hit the country since 1900.(Image credit: Juan Barreto)

items.title: Venezuela declares state of emergency after back-to-back earthquakes leave dozens dead
items.standfirst: Venezuela's interim leader declared a state of emergency on Wednesday after two powerful earthquakes and more than 20 aftershocks struck the country, damaging infrastructure and forcing the closure of Caracas' main airport. Acting President Delcy Rodriguez said the 7.2- and 7.5-magnitude quakes killed at least 32 people and injured more than 700.
items.content: Venezuela's interim leader declared a state of emergency on Wednesday after two powerful earthquakes and more than 20 aftershocks struck the country, damaging infrastructure and forcing the closure of Caracas' main airport. Acting President Delcy Rodriguez said the 7.2- and 7.5-magnitude quakes killed at least 32 people and injured more than 700.

items.title: Venezuela struck by back-to-back earthquakes, many casualties feared

items.content: US Geological Survey says casualties could range from 10,000 to 100,000 people.

items.content: US president singles out alliance members, including the UK, after meeting secretary-general Mark Rutte

items.title: 'I don't think it was us': Trump gives update on investigation into Iran school strike
items.standfirst: President Trump says he doesn't think that the U.S. was responsible for a deadly strike on a school in Iran at the beginning of the war. "I don't think it was us," Trump tells reporters during an Oval Office briefing alongside NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte.
items.content: President Trump says he doesn't think that the U.S. was responsible for a deadly strike on a school in Iran at the beginning of the war. "I don't think it was us," Trump tells reporters during an Oval Office briefing alongside NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte.

items.title: Trump says NATO allies ‘let down’ US by not backing Iran war
items.standfirst: Trump told NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte on Wednesday he was disappointed that alliance members did not back the US military campaign against Iran, saying Washington had "demolished" Iran without needing assistance. Rutte defended allies, noting that 4,000-5,000 US aircraft operated from bases in Europe during the conflict.
items.content: Trump told NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte on Wednesday he was disappointed that alliance members did not back the US military campaign against Iran, saying Washington had "demolished" Iran without needing assistance. Rutte defended allies, noting that 4,000-5,000 US aircraft operated from bases in Europe during the conflict.

items.content: President Trump hosted NATO Secretary-General Mark Rutte at the White House on Wednesday. The pair discussed defense spending and more.

items.title: World Cup 2026: Vinicius Junior scores twice as Brazil beat Scotland to reach last 32
items.standfirst: Vinicius Junior scored twice as Brazil eased to a 3-0 win over Scotland on Wednesday, sealing top spot in Group C and a place in the World Cup last 32. Matheus Cunha added a third, while Neymar made his first international appearance in more than two-and-a-half years after returning from injury.
items.content: Vinicius Junior scored twice as Brazil eased to a 3-0 win over Scotland on Wednesday, sealing top spot in Group C and a place in the World Cup last 32. clusters.title: Trump accuses big oil firms of price-gouging drivers
clusters.neutral_headline: Trump accuses big oil firms of price-gouging drivers
bullet_summary: “The big Oil Companies are not dropping their price at the pump commensurate with the sharply lower prices they are paying for Oil,” the president said
Reported by BBC Top Stories, Guardian Business, NBC World

items.title: Trump accuses big oil firms of price-gouging drivers
items.standfirst: The US president named Shell, ExxonMobil, BP and Chevron when talking to reporters about a probe into fuel prices.
items.content: The US president named Shell, ExxonMobil, BP and Chevron when talking to reporters about a probe into fuel prices.

items.title: Trump says he’s ordered investigation into oil companies over alleged price gouging
items.standfirst: Trump says he instructed justice department to investigate oil firms over high gas prices amid Middle East conflictDonald Trump said on Wednesday that he had instructed the US Department of Justice to investigate oil companies for alleged price gouging, accusing them of not lowering gas prices enough amid conflict in the Middle East.“The big oil companies are not dropping their price at the pump commensurate with the sharply lower prices they are paying for oil. Those prices are dropping like a rock! In other words, customers are being ‘gouged.’ I have instructed the DOJ to immediately start looking into this,” Trump wrote in a social media post late on Tuesday night. “Gasoline prices better start going down a lot faster than what I’m seeing!” Continue reading...
items.content: Trump says he instructed justice department to investigate oil firms over high gas prices amid Middle East conflictDonald Trump said on Wednesday that he had instructed the US Department of Justice to investigate oil companies for alleged price gouging, accusing them of not lowering gas prices enough amid conflict in the Middle East.“The big oil companies are not dropping their price at the pump commensurate with the sharply lower prices they are paying for oil. Those prices are dropping like a rock! In other words, customers are being ‘gouged.’ I have instructed the DOJ to immediately start looking into this,” Trump wrote in a social media post late on Tuesday night. “Gasoline prices better start going down a lot faster than what I’m seeing!” Continue reading...

items.title: Colombia’s leftwing candidate concedes election to Trump-endorsed millionaire
items.standfirst: Leftist Iván Cepeda conceded to far-right lawyer Abelardo de la Espriella who won by razor-thin marginThe defeated leftwing candidate in Colombia’s presidential runoff has conceded to the far-right, Trump-admiring millionaire lawyer Abelardo de la Espriella.Since Sunday night, the preliminary count had already pointed to a De la Espriella victory by a razor-thin margin of less than 1% of the vote. Continue reading...

clusters.title: World Cup 2026: South Africa reach knockout stage for first time with win over South Korea
clusters.neutral_headline: South Africa stun South Korea to reach World Cup knockouts for the first time

items.title: World Cup 2026: South Africa reach knockout stage for first time with win over South Korea
items.standfirst: South Africa reached the World Cup knockout stage for the first time on Wednesday with a 1-0 victory over South Korea, completing a remarkable recovery after losing their opener. Thapelo Maseko scored the decisive second-half goal as Bafana Bafana secured second place in Group A behind Mexico after drawing with the Czech Republic.
items.content: South Africa reached the World Cup knockout stage for the first time on Wednesday with a 1-0 victory over South Korea, completing a remarkable recovery after losing their opener. Thapelo Maseko scored the decisive second-half goal as Bafana Bafana secured second place in Group A behind Mexico after drawing with the Czech Republic.

items.title: South Africa stun South Korea to reach World Cup knockouts for the first time

items.content: Bafana Bafana, playing in the tournament for the first time since they hosted it in 2010, have reached the round of 32.

items.content: Alibaba Group Holding Ltd. shares slid to a 16-month low in Hong Kong after Anthropic PBC accused the Chinese technology giant of “illicitly” accessing its artificial intelligence model.

items.title: Anthropic accuses Chinese rival Alibaba of illicitly extracting AI capabilities
items.standfirst: The firm alleged that Alibaba used fraudulent accounts to access data from its Claude AI model.
items.content: The firm alleged that Alibaba used fraudulent accounts to access data from its Claude AI model.

items.title: Anthropic Accuses Alibaba of ‘Illicitly’ Accessing AI Models
items.standfirst: Anthropic PBC accused Chinese technology giant Alibaba Group Holding Ltd. of waging a large-scale effort to “illicitly” access its Claude artificial intelligence model using thousands of fraudulent accounts that undermine the US AI developer’s decision to keep its products out of China.
items.content: Anthropic PBC accused Chinese technology giant Alibaba Group Holding Ltd. of waging a large-scale effort to “illicitly” access its Claude artificial intelligence model using thousands of fraudulent accounts that undermine the US AI developer’s decision to keep its products out of China.

items.content: The rare school shooting that left three students killed and 20 others injured has shocked the country.

items.title: Philippines blocks GoreBox gaming app after school shooting kills three
items.standfirst: Investigators says one of two teenagers who allegedly opened fire on students at high school was regular playerPhilippine authorities have temporarily blocked the online gaming app GoreBox days after a rare school shooting in the south-east Asian country killed three students and injured 20 others.Investigators said that one of the two teenagers accused of opening fire on students at San Jose National high school in Tacloban city had regularly played the game, which allows players to use various weapons and depicts graphic violence. Continue reading...

clusters.title: Pakistan police rescue French woman and children allegedly held captive by husband for 12 years
clusters.neutral_headline: French woman allegedly held captive by husband for 12 years rescued in Pakistan
bullet_summary: Reported by Guardian World, BBC World

items.title: Pakistan police rescue French woman and children allegedly held captive by husband for 12 years
items.standfirst: Husband arrested after Sylvie Yasmina, 54, and five children found at home in north-western provincePakistan police say they have rescued a French woman and her five children after she told authorities she had been held captive by her husband for more than a decade and subjected to years of domestic abuse in the country’s north-west.The woman, identified as 54-year-old Sylvie Yasmina, was rescued earlier this week from a mud-brick home in Bara, a town in Khyber Pakhtunkhwa province near the Afghan border, the district police chief, Waqar Ahmad, said. Continue reading...
items.content: Husband arrested after Sylvie Yasmina, 54, and five children found at home in north-western provincePakistan police say they have rescued a French woman and her five children after she told authorities she had been held captive by her husband for more than a decade and subjected to years of domestic abuse in the country’s north-west.The woman, identified as 54-year-old Sylvie Yasmina, was rescued earlier this week from a mud-brick home in Bara, a town in Khyber Pakhtunkhwa province near the Afghan border, the district police chief, Waqar Ahmad, said. Continue reading...

items.title: French woman allegedly held captive by husband for 12 years rescued in Pakistan
items.standfirst: Yasmina alleges she was held along with her five children, and they were all cut off from the outside world.
items.content: Yasmina alleges she was held along with her five children, and they were all cut off from the outside world.

items.content: The French doctor had been working in DR Congo, where more than 260 people are known to have died.
